title: Mogorium multiflorum
taxonomy: Mogorium multiflorum is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Acanthaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus family. The Acanthaceae family is part of the order Lamiales, a large order of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Lamiales order is part of the clade asterids, which is one of the two main clades of the angiosperms, the flowering plants. The asterids clade is also known as the euasterids I clade. The classification of Mogorium multiflorum is based on the principles of plant taxonomy and the evolutionary relationships between different plant groups.

Its roots are often employed in traditional remedies to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. In some regions, the plant is believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for infections.
